name: ElevenLabs
description:
ElevenLabs is an AI voice generation platform that converts written text into highly realistic speech, with a particular focus on natural delivery, emotional expression and multilingual support. It has become one of the most widely recognised tools in the AI audio space thanks to the quality and flexibility of its voice models.

Beyond standard text-to-speech, ElevenLabs allows users to create custom voices, clone existing voices and generate narration in dozens of languages. The platform gives users control over pacing, tone and delivery, making it suitable for everything from short-form content and podcasts to audiobooks, training materials and video production.

Its browser-based interface makes voice generation accessible to beginners, while developer APIs and advanced voice management features provide the depth required by businesses, studios and software teams building audio into their products and workflows.
subtitle: AI voice and speech synthesis for creators.
features: Realistic voice generation with human-like timbre
Emotion-controlled speech synthesis
Custom voice cloning
Automatic dubbing & multilingual support
Browser-based editor

use_cases.title: Podcast Production
use_cases.description: Creators can generate narrations, intros, ads, or full episodes using natural-sounding voices. ElevenLabs helps speed up workflow and reduce costly studio sessions.

use_cases.title: Video Narration & YouTube
use_cases.description: YouTubers can produce high-quality narration in multiple voices and languages, enabling faster content creation and consistency without needing to record audio manually.

use_cases.title: Character Voices for Games
use_cases.description: Indie and professional game developers can quickly prototype characters using unique AI-generated voice profiles, complete with emotions and dynamic tone.

pricing: Free plan available — paid plans start at $5/mo

name: Adobe Podcast
description:
Adobe Podcast is a web-based audio recording and editing platform that uses AI to simplify podcast production, voiceovers and spoken-word content. Developed by Adobe, it is designed to make high-quality audio creation more accessible by combining recording, transcription, enhancement and editing tools within a single browser-based workspace.

One of its most notable features is Enhance Speech, which reduces background noise, echo and other recording imperfections to improve vocal clarity. Adobe Podcast also includes remote recording for multiple participants, automatic transcription, text-based editing and support for both audio and video projects. Rather than working directly with waveforms, users can edit recordings by modifying the transcript, making audio production considerably easier for non-specialists.

The platform is aimed at podcasters, content creators, educators, marketers and businesses producing spoken content. Its browser-based interface lowers the barrier to entry for beginners, while multitrack workflows, downloadable speaker tracks and premium features provide additional flexibility for more experienced users and professional teams.
subtitle: AI-enhanced audio cleanup and podcast production tools.
features: AI speech enhancement for studio-quality audio
Noise reduction, echo removal & leveling
Transcript-based editing workflow
Automatic clip detection & cleanup
Browser-based recording studio

use_cases.title: Podcast Editing
use_cases.description: Creators can remove background noise, clean up poorly recorded audio and edit episodes using the text-based editor to save hours of manual work.

use_cases.title: Remote Interviews
use_cases.description: Hosts can record high-quality audio from guests anywhere and apply AI enhancement to ensure consistent sound across all voices.

use_cases.title: Marketing & Business Audio
use_cases.description: Teams can generate clean voiceovers for ads, tutorials and corporate content without needing professional studio equipment.

pricing: Included with Adobe Creative Cloud — Enhance Speech has a free tier.

name: Cleanvoice AI
description:
Cleanvoice AI is an AI-powered audio editing platform designed to streamline the post-production process for podcasts, interviews and other spoken-word recordings. The platform focuses on automatically identifying and removing common speech distractions such as filler words, long pauses, mouth sounds and repeated phrases, helping creators produce cleaner audio without extensive manual editing.

Beyond basic cleanup, Cleanvoice AI offers features for generating transcripts, creating summaries and producing show notes from recorded content. It can also detect and reduce background noise, helping improve overall audio quality while preserving the natural flow of conversation. By automating many of the most time-consuming editing tasks, the platform allows users to spend less time refining recordings and more time creating content.

Cleanvoice AI is primarily used by podcasters, content creators, agencies and media teams that regularly publish spoken content. Its web-based interface makes it accessible to users with little audio-editing experience, while integrations and workflow automation features support larger production environments and recurring publishing schedules.
subtitle: Automatic audio cleanup powered by AI.
features: Automatic filler word removal
Mouth noise detection & cleanup
Stutter & repetition removal
Background noise reduction
Multilingual support

use_cases.title: Podcast Post-Production
use_cases.description: Speeds up editing by automatically removing ums, ahs, lip smacks and awkward pauses for a professional, smooth final cut.

use_cases.title: Voiceover Optimization
use_cases.description: Creators can polish narration for YouTube, audiobooks, or corporate training without manual audio engineering.

use_cases.title: Interview Cleanup
use_cases.description: Multi-speaker recordings become clearer by eliminating distractions such as background noise or verbal tics.

pricing: Pay-as-you-go and subscription plans available from €10/month.

name: Resemble AI
description:
Resemble AI is an AI voice generation platform that enables organisations to create, customise and deploy synthetic voices for a wide range of audio applications. The platform is particularly known for its voice cloning technology, allowing users to generate realistic speech based on existing voice samples while maintaining control over tone, style and delivery.

In addition to text-to-speech generation, Resemble AI provides tools for creating custom voice models, multilingual speech synthesis and real-time voice generation. The platform also offers voice conversion capabilities, enabling one voice to be transformed into another while preserving the original speech content. Features such as emotion control, developer APIs and real-time audio processing make it suitable for interactive applications, virtual assistants, games and customer-facing systems.

Resemble AI is used by developers, media companies, studios and enterprise teams building voice-enabled products and services. Its browser-based tools support voice creation and management, while extensive APIs and integration options allow organisations to incorporate synthetic speech directly into software, content production workflows and communication platforms.
subtitle: Custom voice cloning and AI speech generation.
features: Custom voice cloning
Emotion-driven speech generation
Instant voice conversion
Multilingual & cross-language support
Secure enterprise voice models

use_cases.title: Brand Voice Development
use_cases.description: Companies can create consistent, recognizable synthetic voices for advertisements, virtual agents, and branded experiences.

use_cases.title: Character Voice Creation
use_cases.description: Game studios and filmmakers generate unique voices for characters without hiring multiple voice actors.

use_cases.title: AI Assistants & IVR Systems
use_cases.description: Businesses enhance customer support systems with expressive, natural-sounding automated voices.

pricing: Usage-based pricing with custom enterprise plans.

name: Podcastle AI
description:
Podcastle is an AI-powered audio and video creation platform that helps users record, edit and publish content from a single workspace. Originally focused on podcast production, the platform has expanded to support a broader range of content creation tasks, including voiceovers, interviews, video editing and AI-generated speech. Its combination of recording, editing and publishing tools makes it a popular option for creators looking to manage the entire production process in one place.

Alongside its recording and editing features, Podcastle offers a library of AI voices for generating narration from text. Users can create voiceovers in different styles and accents, clone voices with permission and enhance audio quality using automated cleanup tools. The platform also includes transcription, text-based editing and remote recording capabilities, reducing the need for specialised audio-production software.

Podcastle is used by podcasters, marketers, educators, businesses and independent creators producing spoken and video content. Its browser-based interface is approachable for beginners, while collaboration features, advanced editing tools and API access provide additional flexibility for professional teams and larger content workflows.
subtitle: All-in-one AI platform for podcast creation.

Multitrack recording & editing
Auto-transcription
Remote interview recording

name: ChatGPT
description:
ChatGPT is a conversational AI platform developed by OpenAI that helps users generate text, answer questions, analyse information and assist with a wide range of personal and professional tasks. As one of the most widely used AI assistants, it combines natural language conversation with advanced reasoning, making complex technology accessible through a simple chat interface.

Beyond text generation, ChatGPT can help with writing, research, coding, brainstorming, summarisation and data analysis. It supports file uploads, image understanding, web search and voice interaction, allowing users to work with different types of information within a single platform. The system can also be extended through custom tools, integrations and specialised AI assistants for specific tasks.

ChatGPT is used by individuals, students, professionals and organisations across a wide range of industries. Its straightforward interface makes it approachable for beginners, while advanced capabilities, business plans, APIs and enterprise features provide the flexibility required for teams building AI into their workflows and applications.

name: Google Gemini
description:
Google Gemini is a conversational AI assistant developed by Google that helps users generate content, answer questions, analyse information and complete a wide variety of everyday tasks. Built on Google's Gemini family of AI models, the platform combines natural language interaction with access to Google's wider ecosystem, making it a central part of the company's approach to AI-powered productivity and information discovery.

Gemini can assist with writing, research, brainstorming, coding and summarisation, while also supporting image understanding, document analysis and multimodal conversations that combine text, images and other forms of input. The platform is closely integrated with Google services, allowing users to interact with information from tools such as Gmail, Google Drive, Docs and Maps where supported. It is available through both a web interface and mobile applications, providing a consistent experience across devices.

The platform is used by students, professionals, businesses and everyday consumers looking for AI assistance across a range of tasks. Its familiar interface makes it accessible to new users, while advanced model options, workspace integrations and developer services provide additional capabilities for organisations and software teams.

name: Meta AI
description:
Meta AI is a conversational AI assistant developed by Meta that provides users with help across tasks such as answering questions, generating content, brainstorming ideas and finding information. The platform is built on Meta’s family of large language models and is designed to bring AI assistance into the company’s ecosystem of social, messaging and consumer applications.

In addition to text-based conversations, Meta AI supports image generation and can assist with creative tasks, research, planning and everyday information requests. A key aspect of the platform is its integration across Meta products, including messaging and social media services, allowing AI features to be accessed within familiar communication environments. The assistant is also available through a dedicated web interface, providing a standalone experience outside of Meta’s apps.

Meta AI is aimed at both casual users and professionals seeking a general-purpose AI assistant. Its conversational design makes it easy to use for beginners, while ongoing model development, developer tools and integration capabilities support businesses and software teams exploring AI-powered applications and services.

pricing: Free for Meta platform users.

name: Perplexity
description:
Perplexity is an AI-powered answer engine that combines conversational search with real-time information retrieval from the web. Rather than providing responses based solely on pre-trained knowledge, the platform searches online sources as it generates answers, making it particularly useful for research, fact-finding and staying up to date with current information. Its emphasis on transparency and source attribution has helped distinguish it from many traditional AI chatbots.

The platform can answer questions, summarise complex topics, compare information from multiple sources and assist with research across a wide range of subjects. Responses typically include citations to the sources used, allowing users to verify information and explore topics in greater depth. Perplexity also supports follow-up questions, enabling conversations that build on previous answers while maintaining access to current web content.

Perplexity is used by students, researchers, professionals and everyday users looking for a faster way to gather information online. Its straightforward search-focused interface makes it accessible to beginners, while advanced search modes and API offerings provide additional capabilities for power users and organisations.

Deep research mode
Document analysis & uploads
Follow-up conversational queries

use_cases.title: Market & Industry Analysis

name: Claude
description:
Claude is a conversational AI assistant developed by Anthropic that helps users with writing, analysis, research, coding and a wide range of knowledge-based tasks. Known for its strong performance on long-form reasoning and document-based work, Claude is designed to handle large amounts of information within a single conversation, making it particularly useful for projects that require sustained context and detailed analysis.

The platform can summarise lengthy documents, generate and edit written content, assist with software development, analyse files and answer complex questions. Claude supports large context windows, allowing users to work with extensive reports, codebases and research materials without needing to split information across multiple interactions. It also provides tools for document uploads and structured analysis, helping users extract insights from large volumes of text.

Claude is used by individuals, professionals, researchers and businesses seeking an AI assistant for both everyday tasks and more demanding analytical work. Its clean, conversational interface makes it approachable for new users, while API access and enterprise features enable organisations to integrate Claude into internal tools, workflows and customer-facing applications.
subtitle: Safe, reliable AI assistant for writing and reasoning.

name: Codex AI
description:
Codex is an AI coding assistant developed by OpenAI that helps software developers write, review and manage code through natural language instructions. Originally known as the technology behind early AI coding tools, Codex has evolved into a dedicated coding agent capable of handling complex software engineering tasks, from building new features to analysing large codebases and identifying potential issues.

Beyond code generation, Codex can assist with debugging, code reviews, refactoring, testing and technical documentation. It is designed to work across multiple development environments, including terminals, code editors and cloud-based workspaces, allowing developers to delegate tasks that would otherwise require significant manual effort. The platform can also operate on larger projects, helping teams manage ongoing development work and software maintenance.

Codex is primarily aimed at software engineers, development teams and organisations building software products. It integrates with existing development workflows and offers APIs, automation features and enterprise management tools, making it suitable for both individual developers and larger engineering teams working across complex codebases.

Code explanation & debugging

use_cases.title: Automated Testing

name: Qodo
description:
Qodo is an AI-powered code quality and review platform designed to help software teams improve the reliability, maintainability and consistency of their code. Formerly known as Codium, the platform focuses on the parts of software development that occur after code is written, using AI to review changes, identify potential issues and enforce engineering standards across complex codebases. It is particularly notable for its emphasis on code quality, governance and automated review workflows rather than code generation alone.

The platform provides automated code reviews, pull request analysis, test generation and context-aware feedback directly within development environments and version control systems. Qodo analyses code in the context of the wider codebase, including dependencies, repository history and organisational rules, helping teams detect bugs, security concerns and compliance issues earlier in the development process.

Qodo is primarily aimed at software developers, engineering teams and large organisations managing complex applications. It integrates with popular IDEs, Git platforms and development workflows, while enterprise features support governance, security controls and organisation-wide coding standards.

Language-agnostic support
IDE & CI/CD integration

use_cases.title: Code Review Assistance

use_cases.title: Continuous Integration Quality
use_cases.description: Integrate Qodo into pipelines to enforce consistent code quality standards.

pricing: Professional and enterprise plans available.

name: Stitch
description:
Stitch is an AI-powered interface design platform from Google Labs that helps users turn ideas into application designs and front-end code. Positioned between traditional design tools and AI coding assistants, it aims to reduce the gap between design and development by generating user interfaces from natural language prompts, images, sketches and wireframes. Its focus on rapid prototyping makes it particularly useful during the early stages of product development.

The platform can generate web and mobile interface designs, create multiple design variations and export front-end code based on the generated layouts. Users can refine designs through conversational prompts, upload reference images for inspiration and export projects to tools such as Figma for further editing and collaboration. More recent versions also support interactive prototyping and AI-assisted design iteration.

Stitch is used by designers, developers, product teams and founders looking to move from concept to prototype more quickly. Its visual interface makes it approachable for non-designers, while code export, workflow integrations and developer tooling provide value for professional software teams.

name: Blackbox AI
description:
Blackbox AI is an AI-powered software development platform designed to help developers write, understand and manage code more efficiently. Unlike general-purpose AI assistants, Blackbox AI focuses specifically on programming tasks, providing coding assistance directly within development environments, browsers and dedicated coding tools. It has gained popularity for its combination of code generation, repository analysis and autonomous coding agents that can work across large software projects.

The platform can generate code, explain existing codebases, review pull requests, identify bugs and assist with testing, refactoring and documentation. Blackbox AI also offers autonomous coding agents capable of analysing projects, implementing features and suggesting improvements across multiple files. Integration with popular IDEs and support for numerous programming languages allow it to fit into existing development workflows.

Blackbox AI is primarily used by software developers, engineering teams and technology companies working on applications of varying sizes. Its browser tools make it accessible to individual developers, while IDE integrations, APIs and enterprise-focused features support professional teams managing larger and more complex codebases.

name: Midjourney
description:
Midjourney is an AI image generation platform that creates original artwork and visual content from written prompts. It is widely recognised within the generative AI space for producing highly detailed, stylised and visually distinctive images, making it a popular choice among artists, designers and creative professionals. Rather than focusing solely on realism, Midjourney is particularly known for its strong aesthetic quality and ability to generate imaginative concepts across a wide range of visual styles.

The platform allows users to create images from text descriptions, refine results through iterative prompting and generate multiple variations of a concept. Features such as image references, style controls, upscaling and editing tools provide greater creative direction, while ongoing model updates continue to expand the range and quality of outputs. Midjourney operates through a conversational workflow that encourages experimentation and visual exploration.

Midjourney is used by designers, illustrators, marketers, content creators and creative teams producing concept art, marketing assets and visual inspiration. Its prompt-based interface is accessible to beginners, while advanced controls and extensive customisation options offer additional depth for experienced users and professional creative workflows.

name: DALL-E 3
description:
DALL·E 3 is an AI image generation model developed by OpenAI that creates images from written descriptions. Designed to translate natural language prompts into detailed visual outputs, it is particularly notable for its ability to understand complex instructions, capture fine details and accurately represent relationships between multiple elements within a scene. This improved prompt comprehension helps reduce the need for highly specific prompt engineering compared with earlier image generation systems.

The model can generate a wide variety of images, including illustrations, concept art, marketing visuals, diagrams and realistic scenes. DALL·E 3 is capable of interpreting longer and more nuanced descriptions, allowing users to specify style, composition, mood and subject matter in natural language. It also supports iterative refinement through conversational interactions, making it easier to adjust and develop creative ideas over multiple generations.

DALL·E 3 is used by designers, marketers, educators, content creators and businesses seeking to produce visual content quickly. Its integration with ChatGPT makes image generation accessible to beginners, while API access and workflow integrations provide flexibility for developers and organisations incorporating AI-generated imagery into their products and processes.

name: Canva
description:
Canva AI Image Generator is an AI-powered design tool that creates images from written descriptions directly within Canva’s creative platform. Integrated into Canva’s wider design ecosystem, it allows users to generate original visuals without leaving the design workspace, making it particularly useful for creating marketing materials, presentations, social media content and other digital assets.

The tool converts text prompts into images in a range of styles, enabling users to create illustrations, concept artwork, backgrounds and promotional graphics. Generated images can be edited immediately using Canva’s existing design tools, allowing them to be combined with templates, text, branding elements and other visual content. This integration streamlines the process of moving from an idea to a finished design without requiring specialist image-editing software.

Canva AI Image Generator is aimed at marketers, small businesses, educators, content creators and everyday users who need visual content quickly. Its intuitive interface makes image generation accessible to beginners, while Canva’s collaboration features, brand management tools and business-focused functionality support professional teams working on larger design projects.

The platform also includes plagiarism detection and writing enhancement features, providing support throughout the drafting and editing process rather than focusing on a single task.

QuillBot is widely used by students, academics, professionals, marketers and content creators working with written content on a regular basis. Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to casual users, while browser extensions, integrations with common writing tools and premium features provide additional functionality for more demanding academic and professional workflows.

name: Scribe
description:
Scribe is a simple and powerful tool that turns everyday computer tasks into clear step by step guides. Instead of writing instructions manually, you can perform a workflow as usual and Scribe captures each click and typed input, then creates a visual tutorial with screenshots and explanations. This makes it easy to share processes with teammates or clients who need clear guidance.

Many organisations use Scribe to simplify training and onboarding. New hires can follow polished guides that show real screenshots from your systems, which helps them learn faster and with less confusion. It reduces the need for repeated explanations because everyone has access to instructions that are accurate, visual and easy to follow.

Scribe is perfect for documenting business processes, showing how to use software or creating quick support material for coworkers. It saves time for teams that want consistent documentation without spending hoScribe is an AI-powered process documentation platform that automatically creates step-by-step guides as users complete tasks on their computer. Rather than manually taking screenshots and writing instructions, Scribe captures workflows in real time and converts them into structured documentation, making it particularly useful for creating standard operating procedures, training materials and internal knowledge bases.

The platform records actions performed across web applications and desktop software, then generates visual guides complete with screenshots and written instructions. Users can edit steps, add context, redact sensitive information, apply company branding and share guides through links, PDFs or embedded documentation. Scribe also uses AI to generate titles, descriptions and longer process documents, helping teams maintain documentation as workflows evolve.

Scribe is widely used by operations teams, customer support departments, trainers, IT teams and businesses that need to document repeatable processes. Its automatic capture system makes it accessible to non-technical users, while integrations, analytics and enterprise governance features support larger organisations managing knowledge at scale.urs building manuals or recording long videos. Whether you work in operations, HR or customer support, Scribe helps you turn routine tasks into reliable guides that anyone can understand.

Its focus on academic content sets it apart from general-purpose AI summarisation tools, making it particularly useful for research-heavy workflows.

The platform analyses documents and generates interactive summary flashcards that highlight key findings, methods, results, limitations and conclusions. Scholarcy can extract tables, references and important concepts, while also providing research comparisons, quality indicators and tools for organising notes and annotations. Support for PDFs, online articles, book chapters, Zotero libraries and other sources allows users to consolidate research from multiple locations into a single workspace.

Scholarcy is primarily used by students, researchers, academics and knowledge professionals who regularly work with large volumes of reading material. Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to undergraduates, while advanced analysis, citation management and export features provide valuable support for postgraduate research and professional academic work.

name: Numer AI
description:
Numerai is a crowdsourced AI and quantitative finance platform that allows data scientists to build machine learning models designed to predict stock market performance. Unlike traditional investment platforms, Numerai provides participants with anonymised financial data and combines thousands of independently developed models into a single meta-model that helps power its hedge fund. This unusual approach has made it one of the most distinctive applications of AI within the investment industry.

Participants download datasets, train predictive models and submit their forecasts to Numerai’s weekly tournaments. The platform evaluates these predictions and rewards successful contributors, while its Numeraire (NMR) cryptocurrency can be staked on model performance to align incentives with real-world results. Numerai also operates Numerai Signals, which allows users to submit predictions derived from their own datasets and research.

Numerai is primarily aimed at data scientists, machine learning engineers and quantitative researchers interested in financial modelling. Its open competition structure makes participation accessible to individuals worldwide, while the underlying hedge fund and prediction infrastructure provide a practical environment for applying advanced machine learning techniques to real financial markets.

OpenRead also offers paper comparison tools, citation and relationship graphs, note-taking capabilities and interactive document analysis, helping users navigate complex research topics more effectively.

OpenRead is primarily used by students, researchers, academics and research teams conducting literature reviews or managing large volumes of reading material. Its intuitive interface makes it accessible to newcomers, while collaborative workspaces, research visualisation tools and advanced AI analysis features provide additional value for more experienced researchers and institutions.

Its focus on improving comprehension makes it particularly useful for navigating unfamiliar subjects and highly specialised academic literature.

Users can upload research papers and highlight specific sections to receive plain-language explanations generated in context. The platform also supports follow-up questions, paper summaries and AI-generated insights that identify key findings, important concepts and the overall structure of a paper. By keeping explanations tied to the original document, Explainpaper helps readers explore complex topics without constantly switching between sources.

Explainpaper is primarily used by students, researchers, academics and professionals who regularly work with scholarly literature. Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to newcomers, while features such as library management, saved annotations and research-focused workflows provide additional value for more experienced academic users.

Rather than acting as a general-purpose chatbot, Elicit is built specifically to support structured research workflows and help users work through large volumes of scholarly content more efficiently.

The platform can search academic papers, generate summaries, extract key findings and compare evidence across multiple sources. Users can ask research questions in natural language and receive results drawn from relevant literature, alongside structured information such as study methods, sample sizes and conclusions. Elicit also helps identify gaps in the evidence, organise research findings and automate parts of the literature review process that would otherwise require significant manual effort.

Elicit is used by researchers, students, academics, policy analysts and professionals who rely on evidence-based decision-making. Its intuitive interface makes academic research more accessible to newcomers, while advanced analysis and synthesis tools provide valuable support for experienced researchers working on complex projects.

name: AlphaFold
description:
AlphaFold is an AI system developed by Google DeepMind that predicts the three-dimensional structure of proteins from their amino acid sequences. Widely regarded as one of the most significant applications of AI in scientific research, it addresses a longstanding challenge in biology by enabling researchers to determine protein structures in minutes rather than through lengthy and costly laboratory experiments.

The platform can predict protein structures with a level of accuracy that is often comparable to experimental methods and has expanded to model how proteins interact with other molecules, including DNA, RNA and potential drug compounds. Through the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database, researchers can access hundreds of millions of predicted protein structures, providing an unprecedented resource for biological and medical research.

AlphaFold is primarily used by biologists, biomedical researchers, pharmaceutical companies and academic institutions. Its predictions are helping to accelerate research in areas such as drug discovery, disease understanding, vaccine development and biotechnology, making it an increasingly important tool across the life sciences.

SciSpace is used by students, academics, researchers and industry professionals working with scientific literature. Labguru also incorporates AI-powered tools for protocol generation, data interpretation and workflow automation, helping scientific teams manage complex research activities more efficiently.

Labguru is primarily used by academic laboratories, biotechnology companies, pharmaceutical organisations and research institutions. Its user-friendly interface makes it accessible to researchers and laboratory staff, while customisation options, APIs, compliance features and enterprise-grade controls support larger organisations operating in regulated scientific environments.

name: Pinecone AI
description:
Pinecone is a vector database platform designed to help developers build AI applications that can search, retrieve and use information efficiently. Rather than storing data in traditional database formats, Pinecone specialises in managing vector embeddings, numerical representations that allow AI systems to understand meaning, context and similarity. It has become one of the most widely used infrastructure tools for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), semantic search and AI agents.

The platform enables organisations to store, index and search vast amounts of unstructured data, including documents, knowledge bases and application data. Pinecone supports semantic search, hybrid search, metadata filtering and real-time indexing, helping AI systems retrieve relevant information quickly and accurately. Its fully managed architecture removes much of the complexity associated with scaling vector search infrastructure and maintaining performance as datasets grow.

Pinecone is primarily used by software developers, AI engineers and organisations building AI-powered products. It integrates with popular AI models, frameworks and development tools, while enterprise features such as security controls, compliance certifications and cloud deployment options make it suitable for production-scale applications and large business environments.

Its focus on simplifying complex spreadsheet tasks makes it particularly useful for users who work with data regularly but may not be spreadsheet experts.

The platform can generate Excel and Google Sheets formulas, SQL queries, regular expressions, VBA macros and automation scripts from natural language prompts. It can also explain existing formulas, helping users understand how calculations work and troubleshoot issues more effectively. By translating business requirements into technical spreadsheet logic, GPTExcel reduces the time spent searching for functions and building formulas manually.

GPTExcel is used by analysts, finance professionals, operations teams, students and business users who rely on spreadsheets for reporting and data management. The library includes a wide range of animated UI elements, text effects, backgrounds and interactive components, all available in multiple formats including JavaScript, TypeScript, CSS and Tailwind CSS. Rather than relying on a proprietary framework, React Bits provides source code that developers can modify and adapt to their own projects. Additional tools help users experiment with backgrounds, visual effects and component customisation before implementation.

React Bits is primarily aimed at front-end developers, designers and product teams building React-based applications. Its copy-and-paste approach makes it accessible to individual developers, while extensive customisation options and compatibility with modern development workflows provide flexibility for professional teams and larger web projects.

CodeBeautify is primarily used by software developers, data professionals, students and IT teams who need quick access to technical tools during development and testing. Natively is used by professionals, job seekers, sales teams, consultants and businesses looking to capture and organise information from meetings more effectively. The library supports timelines, keyframes, staggering effects, motion paths and advanced animation sequencing, allowing developers to coordinate complex animations with relatively little code. Anime.js is primarily used by front-end developers, designers and product teams building interactive websites, dashboards and digital experiences. name: InterviewAI
description:
InterviewAI is an AI-powered interview preparation platform designed to help job seekers improve their performance in interviews through realistic practice and personalised feedback. Rather than relying on static question banks, the platform simulates interview conversations and evaluates responses in real time, allowing users to develop both the content and delivery of their answers. Its focus on interactive coaching makes it particularly useful for candidates preparing for competitive roles and career transitions.

The platform can generate role-specific interview questions, conduct mock interviews and provide feedback on areas such as communication, clarity, confidence and answer structure. Users can practise for a range of interview formats, including behavioural, technical and case-based interviews, while receiving suggestions for improvement after each session. The system adapts to different industries and job types, helping candidates prepare for interviews that reflect their target roles more closely.

InterviewAI is used by students, graduates, professionals and job seekers preparing for recruitment processes across a wide range of industries. Its conversational interface makes interview practice more accessible and engaging, while detailed feedback and tailored coaching provide ongoing support for users looking to refine their interviewing skills over time.
subtitle: Personalised mock interviews and feedback to help you ace the real thing.
features: Generates job-specific interview questions across industries and seniority levels
Supports behavioural, technical and situational question types
Instant feedback on structure, clarity and confidence of responses
Session history and analytics to track progress over time
Accessible from web and mobile so users can practice on the go

use_cases.title: Preparing for First-Round Screens

use_cases.title: Career Switching & Returners

name: LogoDiffusion
description:
Logo Diffusion is an AI-powered logo design platform that helps users create, refine and visualise brand identities from text prompts, sketches and existing designs. Built specifically for logo creation, the platform focuses on generating professional branding concepts while giving users greater creative control than many general-purpose image generation tools. Its combination of AI generation and design refinement tools makes it particularly useful during the early stages of brand development.

The platform can generate original logo concepts from written descriptions, transform rough sketches into polished designs and create multiple variations of existing logos. Users can experiment with different styles, layouts, colours and branding directions, while built-in editing tools allow designs to be refined without starting from scratch. Logo Diffusion also includes mock-up generation features, enabling logos to be visualised across packaging, merchandise, websites and other real-world applications.

Logo Diffusion is used by entrepreneurs, designers, agencies, marketers and small businesses creating new brands or exploring visual identity concepts. Its intuitive interface makes logo design accessible to non-designers, while advanced customisation and iteration tools provide additional flexibility for professional branding and creative workflows.
subtitle: Generate distinctive, AI-powered logos and brand marks in minutes.
features: Text-to-logo generation using state-of-the-art diffusion models
Support for style, colour and symbol preferences in prompts
Multiple variations and iterative refinement on chosen concepts
High-resolution exports suitable for web and print use
Simple workflow designed for non-designers and small teams

name: Anara
description:
Anara is an AI-powered research workspace designed to help students, researchers and scientific teams find, understand and write about academic content more efficiently. Built specifically for research workflows, the platform combines literature discovery, document analysis and academic writing tools within a single environment. Its strong emphasis on source-grounded answers and verifiable citations makes it particularly useful for users working with complex scientific and scholarly material.

The platform can analyse research papers, textbooks, lecture materials, audio recordings and other documents, allowing users to ask questions and receive answers linked directly to the original sources. Anara also supports literature reviews, citation discovery, note-taking, collaborative research libraries and AI-assisted writing. Features such as flashcard generation, document annotation and cross-referencing across multiple sources help streamline the research process.

Anara is used by students, academics, scientists and research organisations across a range of disciplines. Its intuitive interface makes it accessible to newcomers, while collaborative workspaces, citation management tools and large-scale document analysis provide the depth required for advanced academic and professional research projects.
subtitle: Turn documents and research into a shared, AI-assisted knowledge workspace.

Ask natural-language questions and receive answers grounded in uploaded sources
Source-linked highlights and citations for trustworthy insights
Collaborative workspaces for teams to share notes and findings
Workflows that reduce manual synthesis time across long documents

name: Claid
description:
Claid is an AI-powered product photography platform designed to help businesses create, edit and enhance product images at scale. Unlike general-purpose image generation tools, Claid focuses specifically on ecommerce and commercial photography, using AI models trained to preserve important product details such as logos, labels, textures and branding. Its specialised approach has made it a popular choice for retailers, marketplaces and brands looking to improve the quality and consistency of their product imagery.

The platform can generate realistic product backgrounds, create lifestyle scenes, remove backgrounds, enhance image quality, upscale photos to higher resolutions and improve lighting and colour balance. Claid also offers AI-generated fashion models, image-to-video capabilities and workflow automation tools that allow large volumes of product images to be processed efficiently through its web platform or API.

Claid is primarily used by ecommerce businesses, online marketplaces, retailers and marketing teams managing large product catalogues. Its browser-based tools make professional product photography more accessible, while API integrations, automation workflows and enterprise services support organisations processing thousands or even millions of images.

name: HireFlow
description:
Hireflow is an AI-powered career platform designed to help job seekers improve their applications and navigate modern recruitment processes more effectively. Best known for its ATS (Applicant Tracking System) resume checker, the platform analyses CVs and resumes to identify formatting issues, keyword gaps and other factors that may affect how applications perform during automated screening. Its focus on practical, application-specific feedback has made it a popular resource for candidates looking to improve their chances of reaching human recruiters.

The platform provides a range of career tools, including resume analysis, job match scoring, cover letter generation, interview preparation and resume-building features. Hireflow uses AI to evaluate resumes against common hiring criteria and offers tailored recommendations to improve relevance, structure and clarity. Many of its tools are designed to deliver feedback quickly, helping users refine applications before submitting them.

Hireflow is primarily aimed at students, graduates and professionals searching for new opportunities across a variety of industries. Its straightforward interface makes it accessible to first-time job seekers, while its application optimisation tools provide ongoing value for experienced candidates applying to competitive roles.
subtitle: Automate sourcing, outreach and candidate workflows in one hiring pipeline.

Dashboards for pipeline health, response rates and time-to-hire

name: eve
type: Legal AI Platform for Plaintiff Firms
description:
Eve is an AI-powered legal technology platform built specifically for plaintiff law firms. Unlike general-purpose AI assistants, it is designed to support the entire lifecycle of a legal case, from client intake and case evaluation through to litigation and resolution. Its focus on plaintiff-side legal work has made it one of the most prominent specialist AI platforms in the legal sector, particularly for personal injury, employment and mass tort law firms.

The platform helps legal teams automate and accelerate time-intensive tasks such as drafting demand letters, generating medical chronologies, conducting legal research, reviewing documents and preparing discovery responses. Eve also provides AI-powered intake tools, case analytics, workflow automation and firm-wide reporting, allowing legal professionals to track case progress and identify opportunities for improvement. A key feature is its ability to generate work product using information from a firm's own case files while providing source references and citations.

Eve is primarily used by plaintiff law firms looking to increase case capacity without significantly expanding headcount. Its specialised legal workflows make it suitable for attorneys, paralegals and legal operations teams, while enterprise security, integrations and practice-wide analytics support larger firms managing high volumes of cases.
subtitle: Trainable legal AI copilot for plaintiff-side case workflows.
features: Configurable AI assistant trained on firm-specific templates and matter types
Support for case intake, triage and conflict checks
Drafting assistance for pleadings, motions and correspondence
Workflow automation for discovery and litigation milestones
Auditability and controls aligned to legal practice requirements

name: Spellbook
description:
Spellbook is an AI-powered contract drafting and review platform designed specifically for legal professionals. Built as an extension for Microsoft Word, it helps lawyers analyse, negotiate and draft contracts without leaving the environment where most legal work already takes place. The platform has become one of the most widely adopted AI tools in the legal sector, particularly among law firms and in-house legal teams focused on commercial contracts.

Spellbook can review contracts against predefined playbooks, identify unusual or potentially risky clauses, suggest redlines and generate new contract language based on legal context and organisational standards. It also allows users to compare agreements against previous contracts, search deal histories and draft clauses or full agreements directly within Word. The platform is tailored specifically to contract work rather than broader legal research or litigation tasks.

Spellbook is primarily used by transactional lawyers, in-house counsel and legal operations teams. Its familiar Word-based workflow makes adoption straightforward, while custom playbooks, precedent libraries and enterprise security features provide the control required by larger legal organisations managing high volumes of contracts.

name: PQAI
description:
PQAI is an AI-powered patent search platform designed to help inventors, researchers and intellectual property professionals discover relevant prior art more efficiently. Developed as an open-source initiative, the platform aims to make patent research more accessible by allowing users to search patent databases using natural language rather than complex keywords, classifications or specialist search techniques. Its focus on semantic search and patent quality has made it a distinctive tool within the intellectual property sector.

The platform analyses invention descriptions and identifies related patents, technical concepts and classification codes based on meaning rather than exact keyword matches. PQAI can surface relevant prior art, highlight key passages within patent documents and help users evaluate the novelty of an idea before investing time and resources in the patenting process. It also offers APIs, export tools and advanced search capabilities for organisations integrating patent intelligence into their own workflows.

PQAI is used by inventors, patent attorneys, research teams, universities and businesses involved in innovation and intellectual property management. Its straightforward search interface makes patent research more approachable for non-specialists, while API access, private deployments and enterprise features support professional IP and R&D environments.

name: Granola
description:
Granola is an AI-powered meeting notes and knowledge management platform designed to help professionals capture, organise and act on information from conversations. Unlike many AI meeting assistants, Granola does not join calls as a visible meeting bot. Instead, it listens locally through a user’s device, combining meeting transcripts with personal notes to create structured summaries, action items and searchable records. This bot-free approach has become one of its most distinctive features.

The platform automatically generates meeting notes, identifies key decisions and next steps, and allows users to search across past conversations. Granola integrates with calendars and works across platforms such as Zoom, Google Meet and Microsoft Teams. It also includes AI-powered chat, meeting briefs, follow-up drafting and integrations that allow meeting insights to flow into other tools and workflows.

Granola is primarily used by professionals in sales, product management, consulting, leadership and other meeting-heavy roles. Its simple interface makes it easy to adopt, while enterprise features such as security controls, administration tools and organisation-wide knowledge sharing support larger teams and businesses.

name: n8n
type: Open Source Workflow Automation & AI Agent Platform
description:
n8n is a workflow automation platform that enables users to connect applications, data sources and AI models through visual workflows. Originally developed as an open-source automation tool, it has become particularly popular for building AI-powered processes and agents that combine large language models with business systems, APIs and human approvals. Its blend of low-code design and developer flexibility makes it a notable alternative to traditional automation platforms.

The platform allows users to automate tasks across hundreds of services, create multi-step workflows and build AI agents that can retrieve information, make decisions and perform actions. Visual workflow builders, custom code support, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), human-in-the-loop controls and extensive integrations enable organisations to create complex automations while maintaining visibility over how they operate. n8n can be deployed in the cloud or self-hosted, giving teams greater control over infrastructure and data.

n8n is used by developers, operations teams, startups and enterprises looking to automate business processes and deploy AI-driven workflows. Its visual interface makes automation accessible to non-specialists, while support for JavaScript, Python, APIs and self-hosting provides the flexibility required for technical teams and large-scale production environments.

name: Make
description:
Make is a visual workflow automation platform that enables users to connect applications, services and data sources without writing extensive code. Formerly known as Integromat, the platform is widely used for automating repetitive business processes, synchronising information between systems and creating complex workflows that span multiple applications. Its visual, flowchart-style interface provides a detailed view of how automations operate, making it particularly appealing to users who want greater control over their workflows.

The platform supports thousands of integrations across categories such as productivity, marketing, ecommerce, customer relationship management and cloud services. Users can build multi-step automations that move data, trigger actions, transform information and coordinate processes across different systems. Make also supports APIs, webhooks, custom logic, scheduling and AI integrations, allowing more advanced workflows to be created without building bespoke software from scratch.

Make is used by marketers, operations teams, entrepreneurs, agencies and businesses looking to reduce manual work and improve process efficiency. Its visual builder makes automation accessible to non-technical users, while advanced routing, data transformation and developer tools provide the flexibility required for complex business and enterprise workflows.

name: Zapier
description:
Zapier is a workflow automation platform that helps users connect applications and automate repetitive tasks without requiring software development expertise. As one of the most widely recognised tools in the automation space, it enables organisations to move information between different services automatically, reducing manual data entry and helping teams streamline everyday processes. Its extensive integration ecosystem has made it a popular choice for businesses of all sizes.

The platform allows users to create automated workflows, known as Zaps, that trigger actions when specific events occur in connected applications. Zapier supports thousands of integrations across categories such as email, customer relationship management, marketing, project management, ecommerce and cloud storage. In addition to traditional automation, the platform now includes AI-powered features, chatbots and AI agents that can analyse information, generate content and perform multi-step tasks across connected systems.

Zapier is used by entrepreneurs, marketers, operations teams, startups and enterprises looking to automate business workflows. Its no-code interface makes automation accessible to beginners, while advanced logic, custom integrations, APIs and administrative controls provide the flexibility required for larger organisations and more complex operational processes.

name: Boomi
description:
Boomi is an integration and automation platform that helps organisations connect applications, data, systems and business processes across complex technology environments. Widely used by enterprises, it provides a unified platform for integrating cloud services, on-premises software and legacy systems, enabling information to flow more efficiently between different parts of an organisation. Its long-standing presence in the integration market has made it a prominent choice for digital transformation and enterprise connectivity projects.

The platform combines application integration, API management, data synchronisation, workflow automation and data governance within a single environment. Organisations can build and manage integrations through visual tools while also supporting more advanced use cases involving large-scale data movement, event-driven architectures and AI-powered automation. Boomi also offers capabilities for master data management, helping businesses maintain consistent information across multiple systems and departments.

Boomi is primarily used by enterprise IT teams, system integrators and organisations operating complex technology stacks. Its low-code approach simplifies integration development, while extensive connectors, governance features and enterprise-grade security provide the scalability and control required for large businesses and regulated industries.

name: Lovable
description:
Lovable is an AI-powered application development platform that enables users to build websites and software products through natural language prompts. Designed to reduce the technical barriers associated with software development, it allows users to describe an idea conversationally and generate working applications with user interfaces, backend functionality and database integrations. Its focus on turning concepts into deployable products quickly has made it one of the most prominent AI-driven app builders.

The platform can generate full-stack web applications, landing pages, internal tools and SaaS products while supporting iterative development through ongoing conversations. Users can modify features, adjust designs, connect external services and refine functionality without manually editing large amounts of code. Lovable also provides integration with development tools and repositories, allowing projects to be customised further and extended beyond the initial AI-generated output.

Lovable is used by founders, entrepreneurs, product teams and businesses looking to prototype or launch software without a traditional development process. Its conversational interface makes application creation accessible to non-technical users, while code access, integrations and collaboration features provide flexibility for developers and teams building more advanced products and workflows.

Hello Atlas is an AI-powered travel planning platform that helps users organise trips through a conversational interface. Rather than requiring travellers to search across multiple websites and booking platforms, it brings itinerary planning, destination research and travel recommendations into a single chat-based experience. Its focus on combining personalised trip planning with live travel information makes it a practical alternative to traditional travel research workflows.

The platform can generate customised itineraries based on factors such as budget, travel style, trip length and preferred destinations. It provides recommendations for flights, accommodation, attractions and activities, while allowing plans to be refined through follow-up conversations. Hello Atlas also incorporates real-time travel information, helping users adapt itineraries as preferences or travel conditions change.

Hello Atlas is aimed at leisure travellers, digital nomads, couples, families and anyone looking to simplify the planning process. Its chat-based design makes it accessible to users with little travel-planning experience, while personalised recommendations and itinerary-building tools provide value for travellers organising more complex trips.

Codiga is a code quality and static analysis platform that helps software developers identify bugs, security vulnerabilities and maintainability issues as they write code. Rather than focusing on code generation, Codiga is designed to improve the quality and safety of existing code through automated analysis, real-time feedback and code review automation. Its emphasis on security and development best practices has made it a useful tool for teams looking to reduce technical debt and catch issues earlier in the development process.

The platform analyses code in real time within development environments and CI/CD pipelines, highlighting issues related to security, performance, code complexity and coding standards. Codiga supports custom analysis rules, automated code reviews, dependency scanning and the detection of common vulnerabilities such as OWASP Top 10 and CWE-related issues. It also provides suggested fixes and reusable code snippets that can be shared across teams.

Codiga is primarily used by software developers, engineering teams and organisations managing large codebases. It integrates with popular IDEs, Git repositories and development workflows, making it suitable for both individual developers seeking immediate feedback and teams implementing organisation-wide code quality standards.

description:
Rows is a spreadsheet platform that combines traditional spreadsheet functionality with built-in AI, data integrations and automation tools. Designed as a modern alternative to conventional spreadsheets, it enables users to analyse data, connect external services and generate insights without relying on multiple separate applications. Its blend of spreadsheet familiarity and AI-powered workflows makes it particularly useful for business users who regularly work with data but do not have specialist technical skills.

The platform supports standard spreadsheet operations while adding features such as AI-assisted analysis, data enrichment, automated reporting and direct integrations with services including databases, marketing platforms and business applications. Users can ask questions about their data in natural language, generate formulas, create reports and automate recurring workflows directly within the spreadsheet environment. This helps reduce the manual effort often involved in data preparation and reporting.

Rows is used by marketers, operations teams, analysts, startups and business professionals looking for a more connected approach to spreadsheet-based work. Its intuitive interface makes advanced data tasks accessible to beginners, while integrations, collaboration features and automation capabilities provide the flexibility required for professional and team-based workflows.
subtitle: A spreadsheet where AI helps you analyse, transform and connect your data.
features: AI Analyst to generate tables, models and formulas from plain-language instructions
Built-in AI functions for summarising, classifying and transforming data
Live data integrations with CRMs, marketing tools, databases and APIs
Shareable dashboards and embeds for reports and internal tools
Modern UX with collaboration, comments and version history

name: SheetAI
description:
SheetAI is an AI-powered extension for Google Sheets that brings large language model capabilities directly into spreadsheet workflows. Rather than requiring users to switch between spreadsheets and separate AI tools, SheetAI allows AI functions to be used within cells, enabling content generation, data processing and analysis from inside a familiar spreadsheet environment. Its tight integration with Google Sheets makes it particularly useful for users who rely heavily on spreadsheets for day-to-day work.

The platform provides custom spreadsheet functions that can generate text, categorise data, summarise information, extract insights and automate repetitive tasks using natural language prompts. Users can apply AI across entire datasets, making it possible to process large volumes of information without building complex formulas or scripts. SheetAI also supports integrations with multiple AI models, giving users flexibility in how AI-powered tasks are performed.

SheetAI is used by marketers, analysts, operations teams, researchers and business professionals who work extensively with spreadsheet data. Its Google Sheets-based approach makes advanced AI functionality accessible to non-technical users, while automation capabilities and scalable workflows provide additional value for teams managing large datasets and recurring processes.
subtitle: Use AI directly in Google Sheets formulas to write, summarise and transform data.

name: Avoma
type: AI Meeting Assistant & Revenue Intelligence Platform
description:
Avoma is an AI-powered meeting intelligence platform that helps teams capture, organise and act on information from conversations. Designed for businesses that rely heavily on meetings, it combines note-taking, transcription, conversation analysis and workflow automation within a single platform. While widely used across organisations, it is particularly popular with sales, customer success and revenue teams that need structured insights from client interactions.

The platform automatically records and transcribes meetings, generates summaries, identifies action items and tracks key topics discussed during conversations. Avoma can analyse trends across meetings, provide coaching insights, highlight customer objections and surface important follow-up tasks. It also integrates with customer relationship management systems, calendars and collaboration tools, allowing meeting data to flow directly into existing business processes.

Avoma is used by sales teams, customer success professionals, managers and business leaders looking to reduce administrative work and improve meeting effectiveness. Its intuitive interface makes meeting intelligence accessible to individual users, while analytics, coaching tools, workflow automation and enterprise integrations support larger organisations managing high volumes of internal and customer-facing conversations.

name: Equal Time
description:
Equal Time is an AI-powered meeting assistant that helps organisations record, analyse and improve virtual and hybrid meetings. While it provides many of the transcription and note-taking capabilities found in modern meeting assistants, its defining focus is on meeting participation, communication patterns and inclusivity. The platform is designed to help teams understand not only what was discussed, but also how conversations were conducted and who contributed to them.

The platform automatically records meetings from Zoom, Microsoft Teams and Google Meet, generating transcripts, summaries, action items and searchable meeting records. Beyond standard meeting intelligence features, Equal Time analyses speaking time, interruptions, participation levels and sentiment, providing insights into meeting dynamics and communication effectiveness. It also includes AI-powered meeting chat, coaching recommendations and workflow integrations for sharing notes across business systems.

Equal Time is used by managers, leadership teams, HR departments, educators and organisations looking to improve collaboration and meeting effectiveness. Its straightforward setup makes it accessible to individual professionals, while analytics, reporting tools and enterprise security features support larger organisations managing communication across distributed teams.
subtitle: See who’s talking, how often—and make your meetings more inclusive with AI insights.

name: Fathom
description:
Fathom is an AI-powered meeting assistant that automatically records, transcribes and summarises virtual meetings. Designed to reduce the administrative burden of note-taking, it captures conversations across major video conferencing platforms and turns them into structured meeting records that can be searched, shared and referenced later. Its combination of accuracy, ease of use and generous free offering has made it one of the most widely adopted AI meeting tools.

The platform generates meeting summaries, action items and key discussion points automatically, allowing users to focus on the conversation rather than taking notes. Fathom also enables users to highlight important moments during meetings, create clips from recordings and share insights with colleagues. Integrations with customer relationship management systems, productivity tools and collaboration platforms help meeting information flow directly into existing workflows.

Fathom is used by sales teams, consultants, managers, customer success professionals and anyone who spends significant time in meetings. Its straightforward setup makes it accessible to individual users, while workflow automation, integrations and team collaboration features provide additional value for organisations managing large volumes of customer and internal conversations.

name: Fellow

Fellow is an AI-powered meeting management platform that helps teams plan, run and follow up on meetings more effectively. Rather than focusing solely on transcription and note-taking, the platform supports the entire meeting lifecycle, from agenda creation and preparation through to action tracking and post-meeting documentation. Its emphasis on structured collaboration and accountability has made it a popular choice for managers, leadership teams and organisations seeking more productive meetings.

The platform provides collaborative agendas, meeting notes, action item tracking and AI-generated summaries that capture key decisions and next steps. Fellow can record and transcribe meetings, generate follow-up content and integrate with calendars, video conferencing platforms and workplace tools. It also supports recurring meeting templates, performance discussions and team check-ins, helping organisations establish consistent meeting practices across departments.

Fellow is used by managers, executives, project teams and businesses that rely on regular collaboration and decision-making meetings. Its intuitive interface makes it easy for teams to adopt, while workflow integrations, analytics and enterprise-grade administration features provide the structure and visibility required by larger organisations.

name: Krisp
type: AI Noise Cancellation & Voice Clarity Tool
description:

Best known for its noise cancellation technology, the platform removes background sounds, echoes and other distractions in real time, helping conversations remain clear even in noisy environments. Its focus on audio quality and communication has made it a widely used tool among remote workers, customer-facing teams and distributed organisations.

Beyond noise suppression, Krisp offers meeting transcription, note-taking, call recording and AI-generated summaries. The platform can automatically capture key discussion points, action items and decisions, allowing users to focus on conversations rather than documentation. It works across a wide range of communication tools, including Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Google Meet and contact centre software, without requiring changes to existing workflows.

Krisp is used by professionals, remote teams, call centres, recruiters and sales organisations that depend on clear communication. Its simple setup makes it accessible to individual users, while enterprise administration, analytics and integration features provide additional value for businesses managing large numbers of calls and meetings.

Its straightforward setup makes automation accessible to beginners, while support for custom workflows, webhooks and business application integrations provides additional flexibility for growing organisations and more advanced use cases.

name: Squirro
description:
Squirro is an enterprise AI and knowledge management platform that helps organisations find, analyse and act on information spread across multiple systems and data sources. Combining enterprise search, AI-powered insights and workflow automation, the platform is designed to turn large volumes of structured and unstructured data into actionable business intelligence. Its strong focus on knowledge-intensive industries has made it particularly popular in sectors such as financial services, manufacturing, healthcare and public services.

The platform connects to internal documents, databases, business applications and external information sources, creating a unified layer for search and analysis. Squirro uses technologies such as enterprise search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), machine learning and AI assistants to deliver answers, recommendations and contextual insights grounded in organisational data. It also supports custom AI agents, workflow automation and industry-specific solutions tailored to different business functions.

Squirro is used by enterprise teams, analysts, knowledge workers and decision-makers who need faster access to information across complex organisations. Its configurable architecture, governance controls and integration capabilities make it well suited to businesses seeking to deploy AI while maintaining security, compliance and oversight of sensitive data and processes.

The platform is flexible enough to handle different workflows and mapping rules for each connection. Teams can control which fields sync, how often updates happen and how conflicts are handled. This ensures each system stays accurate while still fitting local needs.

Exalate is useful for organisations that rely on multiple tools across departments or partners. It keeps everyone aligned while allowing each group to continue using the software that works best for them.
